generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance AutonomousVehicleActuationSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Layered autonomous vehicle actuation system with lower-level chassis for steering/speed control and upper-level autonomy components hosting Jetson Orin Nano, LiDAR, and depth camera"
]

instance MotorVoltageController : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Vedder Electronic Speed Controller converting desired speed commands into motor voltage commands for ACC operation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NVIDIAJetsonOrinNano
]

instance SteeringAngleController : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Steering servo actuation managing vehicle's lower-level chassis steering angle adjustments"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NVIDIAJetsonOrinNano
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlController : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "ACC Controlling FE computing lead vehicle distance and determining desired speed based on ISO 15622 Performance Class I standards via ROS2 topics and Wi-Fi communication"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR generating point cloud data for object detection in vehicle's proximity"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel Deepsense D435i Depth Camera providing 3D environmental context for object tracking and path projection"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraData
]

instance VehicleStateSensors : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Continuous speed and steering angle sensors capturing real-time ego vehicle metrics synchronized with 100ms accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EgoVehicleStateData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction Data Streams
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DBSCANClustering, KalmanFiltering
]

instance DepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera
]

instance EgoVehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensors
]

instance ObjectTrackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARPointCloudData, DepthCameraData
]

// C5: Data Storage
instance RealTimeObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series object tracks (position, velocity, size) generated from processed LiDAR/depth camera data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTrackData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SQLDatabase
]

instance VehicleStateHistory : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical speed and steering angle data synchronized with 100ms accuracy requirement"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EgoVehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SQLDatabase
]

instance PointCloudClusteringResults : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "DBSCAN-processed point cloud clusters for noise filtering and object segmentation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Node
]

// C6: Models/Data Processing
instance DBSCANClustering : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Point cloud clustering algorithm (DBSCAN) for noise filtering and object segmentation from LiDAR data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Node, Python38Implementation
]

instance KalmanFiltering : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Kalman filter for state estimation of detected objects from point cloud data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Node, Python38Implementation
]

instance ObjectTrackingAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Real-time object tracking combining DBSCAN clusters and depth camera data for position/velocity estimation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Node, Python38Implementation
]

instance ISO15622ACCControl : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Performance Class I ACC control logic following ISO 15622 standards for distance-based speed adjustment"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Node, CPlusPlusImplementation
]

instance 3DEnvironmentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D CAD-derived environmental representation for real-time path projection and visualization"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Node, Open3DVisualization
]

// C7: Enablers
instance NVIDIAJetsonOrinNano : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Microcomputer platform hosting ACC control logic and perception processing"
    DTDFVocab:enables ROS2Node, Python38Implementation, CPlusPlusImplementation
]

instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Robot Operating System 2 for inter-component communication between sensors, actuators, and Digital Twin Entity"
    DTDFVocab:enables ROS2Node, DataTranslationFE, WiFiCommunication
]

instance Python38Implementation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python 3.8 environment for rapid prototyping of perception algorithms (clustering, tracking)"
    DTDFVocab:enables DBSCANClustering, KalmanFiltering, ObjectTrackingAlgorithm
]

instance CPlusPlusImplementation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "C++ implementation for ROS2 node lifecycle management and critical control logic"
    DTDFVocab:enables ROS2Node, ISO15622ACCControl
]

instance MATLABSimulink : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink for complex system simulations including point cloud processing and control validation"
    DTDFVocab:enables DBSCANClustering, KalmanFiltering, ISO15622ACCControl
]

instance SQLDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SQL database for storing historical object tracks and vehicle state data externally to vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeObjectTracks, VehicleStateHistory
]

instance Open3DVisualization : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D visualization libraries for real-time rendering of vehicle state and object tracks"
    DTDFVocab:enables 3DVisualizationFE
]

instance DataTranslationFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data Translation Functional Entity ensuring compatibility between ROS2 and Wi-Fi communication protocols"
    DTDFVocab:enables ROS2Node, WiFiCommunication
]

instance WiFiCommunication : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "UDP-based Wi-Fi network communication between vehicle and Digital Twin Entity with 100ms latency"
    DTDFVocab:enables AdaptiveCruiseControlController, RemoteManagementService
]

// C8: Services
instance RealTimeVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "3D virtual cockpit displaying ego vehicle speed, steering angle, detected object tracks, and projected driving path"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTime3DVisualization
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC control logic from vehicle ECUs to cloud-based infrastructure reducing onboard resource constraints"
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteControlLogicExecution
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables operators to enable/disable ACC, set desired speeds, or trigger emergency braking via Control Panel UI (100ms latency)"
    DTDFVocab:provides OperatorRemoteActuation
]

instance ReportGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automatically generates human-readable reports of object tracks and vehicle state as time-series data for troubleshooting"
    DTDFVocab:provides AutomatedReporting
]

// C9: Insights/Actions
instance RealTime3DVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of real-time ego vehicle state, detected objects, and projected driving path in 3D environment"
]

instance RemoteControlLogicExecution : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Execution of ACC control logic offloaded to cloud infrastructure with reduced latency impact on onboard system"
]

instance OperatorRemoteActuation : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote control actions via Control Panel UI (enable/disable ACC, set desired speeds, emergency braking) with 100ms response time"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance AutomatedReporting : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated generation of time-series reports for object tracks and vehicle state data for analysis"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C10: Environment
instance AutonomousVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains AutonomousVehicleActuationSystem,
        HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR,
        IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera,
        VehicleStateSensors,
        ROS2Middleware,
        NVIDIAJetsonOrinNano
]

instance DigitalTwinSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains RealTimeVisualizationService,
        ComputationOffloadingService,
        RemoteManagementService,
        ReportGenerationService,
        SQLDatabase,
        Open3DVisualization
]

// C11: Components
instance PhysicalVehicleChassis : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Lower-level chassis hosting steering and speed control mechanisms for autonomous vehicle"
]

instance AutonomyControlSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Upper-level chassis hosting Jetson Orin Nano, LiDAR, depth camera, and ACC control logic"
]

instance PerceptionProcessingUnit : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Hardware platform (Jetson Orin Nano) executing perception algorithms and control logic"
]

instance ROS2CommunicationNetwork : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Middleware infrastructure enabling inter-component communication between physical and digital entities"
]

instance CloudBasedProcessing : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "External infrastructure handling computation offloading and report generation"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT sends control commands to the physical twin through:
  - **ACC Controlling FE (Digital Twin Entity)**: Computes desired speed based on lead vehicle distance and sends commands via Wi-Fi (UDP) to the vehicle’s Jetson Orin Nano.
  - **Emergency Braking Commands**: Triggered via the Control Panel UI, executed by the Actuation FE to halt the motor.
  - **Remote Management Commands**: Enables/disables ACC or modifies desired speed, transmitted over Wi-Fi with <100 ms latency (3GPP 22.185 compliance)."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin operates with the following time-scale specifications:
  - **Real-time Data Collection**: Data streams (e.g., perception cycles, sensor readings) are processed at real-time rates, with a maximum end-to-end latency of 100 ms between the vehicle and the Digital Twin Entity (per 3GPP 22.185).
  - **Clock Synchronization**: Devices within the DT system must maintain a 100 ms accuracy in time synchronization for event ordering and data integrity, though NTP-based synchronization is not explicitly detailed (only accuracy requirement is specified).
  - **Data Processing Frequency**: Point cloud clustering and tracking occur at unspecified but high-frequency rates (likely >10 Hz), while ACC control updates are synchronized with the perception cycle (real-time, but no exact Hz value is provided)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is structured as a **multi-layered entity-based architecture** adapted from ISO 23247:
  - **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts sensor preprocessing (LiDAR/Depth Camera), actuation (motor/steering), and data translation.
  - **Digital Twin Entity**: Manages data collection, ACC control logic, and report generation.
  - **User Entity**: Provides 3D visualization and control panel interfaces (split into two sub-FEs: Presentation FE and User Interface FE).
  - **Cross-System Entity**: Implements Data Translation FE for inter-entity communication (e.g., ROS2/Wi-Fi protocols). The architecture deviates from ISO 23247 by introducing a **Physical Twin Entity** (not defined in the standard) to encapsulate automotive-specific resources."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates across the following lifecycle stages:
  - **Design Phase**: Used for cyber-physical experimentation and validation of ACC system requirements (e.g., ISO 15622 compliance).
  - **Operational Phase**: Enables real-time monitoring, remote management, and optimization of ACC during vehicle operation.
  - **No explicit Predictive Maintenance Stage**: While predictive maintenance is mentioned as a general DT application in the background, it is not a specific service or lifecycle phase for this ACC case study."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity considerations include:
  - **Verification**: Ensures real-time data streams meet latency (<=100 ms) and clock synchronization (<=100 ms) requirements.
  - **Validation**: Validates ACC logic against ISO 15622 standards and system performance against perception cycle constraints.
  - **Uncertainty Handling**: No explicit safety margins are mentioned, though the system adheres to strict timing constraints for safety-critical operations.
  - **Data Accuracy**: Point cloud processing (DBSCAN/Kalman) and sensor fusion ensure high-fidelity object tracking and ego vehicle state estimation."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs the following technical connections:
  - **Communication Protocols**:
    - **Intra-Entity**: ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2) for intra-entity communication within the vehicle’s onboard system.
    - **Inter-Entity**: Wi-Fi (UDP) for communication between the vehicle and Digital Twin Entity, ensuring low-latency (<100 ms) data transfer.
  - **Clock Synchronization**: Devices must synchronize clocks with 100 ms accuracy (hardware constraint), though NTP-based synchronization is not explicitly detailed.
  - **Data Serialization**: Data Translation FE ensures compatibility across ROS2 and Wi-Fi protocols, including unit conversions and message alignment."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is deployed as follows:
  - **Physical Twin**: Embedded on the 1/10th-scale autonomous vehicle, using NVIDIA Jetson NX/Orin Nano for compute.
  - **Digital Twin Entity**: Hosted on cloud infrastructure (not specified) for offloading ACC control logic.
  - **User Interface**: Deployed on a remote device (e.g., laptop/desktop) via web-based ROS2 clients or desktop applications."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The DT integrates horizontally with:
  - **External Systems**: No explicit mention of integration with other DTs or external IoT systems (e.g., V2V communication). Potential future extensions could include:
    - **Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) Protocols**: Adherence to 3GPP 22.185 for low-latency communication with other autonomous vehicles.
    - **Cloud Services**: Offloading data storage/reporting to external databases."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership is ambiguous but inferred as:
  - **Physical Twin Data**: Owned by the vehicle manufacturer/developer (sensors/actuators).
  - **DT Data**: Collected and processed by the DT service provider (cloud-based storage/reporting). Privacy considerations are not addressed in the document, but real-time data transmission over Wi-Fi may require encryption (not specified)."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT follows **ISO 23247** for architectural design (entity-based structure, Data Translation FE), **ISO 15622** for ACC control logic, **ROS2** for middleware, and **3GPP 22.185** for V2V communication constraints. Deviations include the introduction of a **Physical Twin Entity** (not defined in ISO 23247) and a split User Interface FE."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Safety and security are addressed as follows:
  - **Safety**:
    - **Latency Constraints**: End-to-end latency ≤100 ms ensures deterministic control for ACC (critical for autonomous driving).
    - **Clock Synchronization**: ±100 ms accuracy prevents timing-based attacks or data corruption.
    - **ISO 15622 Compliance**: ACC logic adheres to safety standards for autonomous vehicles.
  - **Security**:
    - **Encryption**: Not explicitly mentioned; potential Wi-Fi communication may require TLS/SSL for data integrity.
    - **Remote Management**: Emergency braking commands are triggered via UI with explicit user consent (human-in-the-loop).
    - **Data Integrity**: Data Translation FE ensures standardized message formats to prevent malformed commands."
]
